qa_pairs.question: How do I retrieve the 2024R2.1 Antennas examples for AnsysEDT?
qa_pairs.answer: First, load the `ansysedt/2024R2.1` module. Then, execute `mkdir -p ~/Ansoft/$EBVERSIONANSYSEDT; rm -rf ~/Ansoft/$EBVERSIONANSYSEDT/Antennas` followed by `cp -a $EBROOTANSYSEDT/v242/Linux64/Examples/HFSS/Antennas ~/Ansoft/$EBVERSIONANSYSEDT` to copy the examples to your account.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to run an Antennas example in AnsysEDT after opening the .aedt file?
qa_pairs.answer: After opening the .aedt file, click `HFSS -> Validation Check`. Then, navigate to `Simulation -> setup -> advanced -> Mesh/Solution options` and select `Use Defaults`. Finally, start the simulation by clicking `Simulation -> Analyze All`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I quit AnsysEDT without saving a converged solution?
qa_pairs.answer: To quit AnsysEDT without saving the converged solution, click `File -> Close -> No`.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if AnsysEDT crashes and won't restart?
qa_pairs.answer: If AnsysEDT crashes and won't restart, run the commands `pkill -9 -u $USER -f "ansys*|mono|mwrpcss|apip-standalone-service"` and `rm -rf ~/.mw`. The latter command will cause AnsysEDT to re-run its first-time configuration on startup.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use the SHARCNET Ansys license for AnsysEDT modules?
qa_pairs.answer: The SHARCNET Ansys license, which includes AnsysEDT, can be used for free by any researcher on any Alliance cluster.

qa_pairs.question: How do I configure my `ansys.lic` file to use the SHARCNET Ansys license?
qa_pairs.answer: Configure your `ansys.lic` file by creating or modifying `$HOME/.licenses/ansys.lic` to contain the following two lines: `setenv("ANSYSLMD_LICENSE_FILE", "1055@license3.sharcnet.ca")` and `setenv("ANSYSLI_SERVERS", "2325@license3.sharcnet.ca")`.
